author: NETWORG
name: Power Platform: Environment Variable Value
description: Adds the default value file (environmentvariablevalues.json) for an existing environment variable definition. The JSON sits next to environmentvariabledefinition.xml inside the definition's folder. Run pp-environment-variable-definition first.

SolutionRootPath.description: Relative path from the project folder (.csproj) to the folder containing unpacked Dataverse solution files (Other/, Entities/, OptionSets/). Use '.' if solution files are directly in the project folder, or a subfolder name like 'Declarations'. Do not use 'Entities' or 'Other' — those are subdirectories inside the solution metadata root.

DefinitionLogicalName.displayName: Schema name of the existing environment variable definition (including publisher prefix)
DefinitionLogicalName.description: Full schema name of the existing environment variable definition (e.g., tom_portal_hostnamemain). Must match the folder created by pp-environment-variable-definition.

Value.description: Value to ship as the default for this environment. For Boolean definitions use 'yes' or 'no'. For JSON definitions supply a valid JSON object as a single string.

EnvironmentVariableValueId.description: Unique identifier for the environment variable value (automatically generated GUID).
